{"id":"oya-frontend-2mn","title":"engine: Implement core workflow execution engine","description":"Implement the core workflow execution engine that runs workflows entirely in the browser via WASM.

## Context
Previous beads completed:
- bd-1c8: ExecutionState enum for node runtime status
- bd-184: Async IO Bridge  
- bd-1cu: Robustness (Cycles & Timeouts)
- bd-1k0: ServiceKind and ContextType enums (OPEN - priority 0)

## Success Criteria
- Workflows execute entirely in the browser's WASM runtime
- Node-to-node data transfer produces correct results
- Execution context is passed between nodes
- Cycles are handled without infinite loops
- Memory usage stays under 50MB for typical workflows

## Scope
- Core execution loop
- Node execution handlers
- Execution context management
- DAG traversal and scheduling
- Error isolation per node
- Timeout handling

## Non-goals
- Server-side execution
- Real-time collaboration
- Complex branching logic (deferred)

## References
- specs/flow-wasm-v1.yaml: workflow-execution behavior
- src/graph/execution.rs: existing patterns","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T19:41:48Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T19:41:48Z"}

{"id":"oya-frontend-btv","title":"restate: Integrate Restate service types and context handling","description":"Integrate Restate service types (ServiceKind and ContextType) into the workflow execution engine.

## Context
Previous beads completed:
- oya-frontend-2mn: Core workflow execution engine
- bd-1c8: ExecutionState enum for node runtime status
- bd-184: Async IO Bridge
- bd-1cu: Robustness (Cycles & Timeouts)

## Success Criteria
- ServiceKind enum properly aligned with Restate SDK
- ContextType enum for workflow vs service contexts
- Node type inference from ServiceKind
- Type-safe connection validation based on service kinds
- All 24 node types properly categorized

## Scope
- Add ServiceKind enum (Service, VirtualObject, Workflow)
- Add ContextType enum (ServiceContext, WorkflowContext)
- Implement type inference from WorkflowNode
- Update connection validation to check ServiceKind compatibility
- Add Restate-specific node validation

## Non-goals
- Actual Restate client implementation (separate bead)
- Service deployment configuration (separate bead)

## References
- docs/10_RESTATE_SDK.md
- specs/flow-wasm-v1.yaml
- src/graph/restate_types.rs","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T21:13:49Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T21:13:49Z"}

## Context

- ContextType enum with 2 variants (Synchronous, Asynchronous)

- check_connection() function with ContextType checking

- Implement ServiceKind, ContextType, PortType enums
- Implement types_compatible() function
- Implement check_connection() function  
- Implement get_node_by_id() helper
- Update WorkflowNode impls for service_kind(), context_type(), input/output_port_type()
- Write unit tests for all types and functions
- Write integration tests for connection validation
- Write proptest invariants
- Write Kani model checking harnesses

## Non-goals
- Actual Restate client code (separate bead)
- Service deployment (separate bead)

Variants like CycleDetected, NodeNotFound, ExecutionFailed, Timeout, MemoryLimitExceeded, InvalidConfig, NoEntryNodes, EmptyWorkflow, InvalidStateTransition are all missing. This is a fundamental contract violation. Fix required: Implement ExecutionError enum with all contract-specified variants.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T21:35:28Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T21:35:28Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"Lewis","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"oya-frontend-duz","title":"[Red Queen] CRITICAL: Timeout enforcement not implemented - contract violation","description":"Adversarial testing revealed that the ExecutionConfig with timeout_ms from the contract is NOT IMPLEMENTED. The execute_workflow function with timeout enforcement does not exist. Nodes can run indefinitely without any timeout. The contract specifies ExecutionConfig.timeout_ms and ExecutionError::Timeout variant but neither is implemented. Fix required: Implement ExecutionConfig, execute_workflow with timeout enforcement, and ExecutionError enum with Timeout variant.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T21:34:54Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T21:34:54Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"oya-frontend-2wt","title":"Architectural decision: ConnectionError enum consolidation","description":"Black Hat Code Review found that the bead created src/graph/connection_errors.rs with a ConnectionError enum, but the existing codebase has src/graph/connectivity.rs with its own ConnectionError enum.

The two enums have different purposes:
- connectivity.rs ConnectionError: Graph connectivity validation (self-connections, cycles, missing nodes, duplicate edges, type mismatches)
- connection_errors.rs ConnectionError: Restate service type validation (port type compatibility, service kind compatibility, context type compatibility)

DECISION REQUIRED: Should these be:
1. Merged into a single unified ConnectionError enum?
2. Kept separate with clear module boundaries?
3. The new connection_errors.rs should replace connectivity.rs entirely?

This bead tracks the architectural decision and implementation of the chosen approach.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-30T01:49:30Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-30T01:49:30Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"Lewis","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"oya-frontend-7me","title":"[Red Queen] CRITICAL: Duplicate node ID validation missing - causes undefined behavior","description":"Adversarial testing revealed that the workflow execution engine does NOT validate unique node IDs. Duplicate node IDs are silently accepted and cause undefined behavior during execution_queue processing. The contract specifies Invariant I1: Unique Node IDs but this is violated. Fix required: Add duplicate node ID validation in validate_workflow() that checks for duplicate IDs in workflow.nodes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T21:34:47Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T21:34:47Z"}

new_value: Fixed: Added validate_unique_node_ids() function to validate_workflow() that checks for duplicate node IDs using HashSet. Returns ValidationIssue with ERROR severity for each duplicate found.

Decided to keep separate ConnectionError enums with clear module boundaries: connectivity::ConnectionError for graph edit-time validation (self-connections, cycles, missing nodes), connection_errors::ConnectionError for Restate service type validation (port types, service kinds, context types). Both exported with distinct aliases (GraphConnectionError/ConnectivityConnectionError vs RestateConnectionError) and ConnectionError re-exported for backward compatibility.
{"assignee":"Lewis","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"oya-frontend-5uz","title":"[Red Queen] CRITICAL: Self-connection cycle detection missing - can cause infinite loops","description":"Adversarial testing revealed that the workflow execution engine does NOT detect self-connections in validate_connection_validity(). Self-connections are silently accepted and can cause infinite loops in execution_queue processing, stack overflow during recursive operations, and undefined behavior in dependency resolution. The contract specifies Invariant I3: No Self-Connections but this is violated. Fix required: Add self-connection validation in validate_connection_validity() that checks if conn.source == conn.target and reports error.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T21:34:40Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T21:34:40Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"Lewis","status":"in_progress"}
old_value:
{"id":"oya-frontend-vxk","title":"Test plan required for bead oya-frontend-btv - Restate types and context handling","description":"Bead oya-frontend-btv ('restate: Integrate Restate service types and context handling') was submitted for final review but has NO test-plan.md file. 

The Test Reviewer skill requires both contract.md and test-plan.md for Mode 1 Plan Inquisition, or implementation + existing tests for Mode 2 Suite Inquisition.

Current state:
- Implementation exists in src/graph/service_kinds.rs
- Tests exist in tests/flow_extender_contracts.rs (but these are for flow_extender module, NOT for the restate_types module)
- NO test-plan.md exists for the bead

The user's fix notes mention:
1. Added ContextTypeMismatch error handling to check_connection()
2. Added get_node_by_id() function that uses NodeNotFound
3. Fixed Node → WorkflowNode in check_connection() signature
4. Fixed wildcard assertions in test plan

BUT: No test-plan.md file exists to verify these claims. No implementation of check_connection() or get_node_by_id() exists in the codebase.

MANDATE: A test-plan.md must be created for bead oya-frontend-btv before it can be approved. The test plan must cover:
- ServiceKind enum parsing (Handler, Workflow, Actor variants)
- ContextType enum parsing (Synchronous, Asynchronous variants, with sync/async aliases)
- ParseServiceKindError error variant with exact error assertions
- ParseContextTypeError error variant with exact error assertions
- ConnectionError::TypeMismatch variant (already has tests in connectivity.rs but need to verify it covers ServiceKind-based validation)
- get_node_by_id() function if it exists
- check_connection() function if it exists

Without a test-plan.md, the bead cannot pass the Plan Inquisition gate.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:50:42Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T23:50:42Z"}

old_value: {"id":"oya-frontend-tfi","title":"[Red Queen] MAJOR: State machine enforcement missing - direct mutation bypasses rules","description":"Adversarial testing revealed that ExecutionState can be directly mutated without any validation. Transitions like Completed -\u003e Running are possible via direct field assignment. The contract specifies Invariant I9: State Machine Compliance but there is no enforcement. The try_transition() function exists but is not used to enforce transitions. Fix required: Either use type-safe state machine (sealed trait pattern) or enforce transitions via try_transition() at all mutation points.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T21:35:08Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T21:35:08Z"}

No error is reported, no warning is generated. The workflow appears to succeed but with missing nodes. Fix required: Either implement proper cycle detection that reports cycles, or implement iterative execution with maximum iteration count and timeout.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T21:35:01Z","created_by":"Lewis","updated_at":"2026-03-29T21:35:01Z"}
